questions.question: What is the solution set for $\frac{2(x-1)}{5} \leq \frac{3(2+x)}{7}$?
questions.question_raw: What is the solution set for {tex}\frac{2(x-1)}{5} \leq \frac{3(2+x)}{7}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\frac{2(x-1)}{5} \leq \frac{3(2+x)}{7}{/tex} 
Multiplying both sides by LCM (5, 7) = 35, we get
{tex}35 \cdot \frac{2(x-1)}{5} \leq 35 \cdot \frac{3(2+x)}{7}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14(x - 1) {tex}\le{/tex} 15(2 + x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14x - 14 {tex}\le{/tex} 30 + 15x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 14x - 15x {tex}\le{/tex} 30 + 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -x {tex}\le{/tex} 44
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\ge{/tex} -44
Solution set is {tex}\left[ -44,\infty \right) {/tex}

questions.question: Identify the solution set for $\frac{(x-1)}{3}+4<\frac{(x-5)}{5}-2$
questions.question_raw: Identify the solution set for {tex}\frac{(x-1)}{3}+4<\frac{(x-5)}{5}-2{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{(x-1)}{3}+4<\frac{(x-5)}{5}-2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-1+12}{3}<\frac{x-5-10}{5}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x+11}{3}<\frac{x-15}{5}{/tex}
Multiplying both sides by LCM (3, 5) = 15, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5(x + 11) < 3(x - 15)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5x + 55 < 3x - 45
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x < -100
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x < -50
So solution set is{tex}( - \infty , - 50){/tex}

questions.question: What is the solution set for $\frac{2 x-3}{3 x-7}>0$?
questions.question_raw: What is the solution set for {tex}\frac{2 x-3}{3 x-7}>0{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2 x-3}{3 x-7}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 3 > 0 and 3x - 7 > 0) or (2x - 3 < 0 and 3x - 7 < 0)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x > {tex}\frac { 3 }{ 2 }{/tex} and x > {tex}\frac { 7 }{ 3 }{/tex}) or (x < {tex}\frac { 3 }{ 2 }{/tex} and x < {tex}\frac { 7 }{ 3 }{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow x>\frac { 7 }{ 3 }{/tex} or {tex}x<\frac { 3 }{ 2 }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x\in \left( \frac { 7 }{ 3 } ,\infty \right) {/tex} or {tex}x\in \left( -\infty ,\frac { 3 }{ 2 } \right){/tex}
Hence the solution set is {tex}\left(-\infty, \frac{3}{2}\right) \cup\left(\frac{7}{3}, \infty\right){/tex}

questions.question: Identify the solution set for $\frac{7 x-5}{8 x+3}>4$
questions.question_raw: Identify the solution set for {tex}\frac{7 x-5}{8 x+3}>4{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{7 x-5}{8 x+3}>4{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{7 x-5}{8 x+3}-4>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{7 x-5-4(8 x+3)}{8 x+3}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{7 x-5-32 x-12}{8 x+3}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{-(25 x+17)}{8 x+3}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{(25 x+17)}{8 x+3}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (25x + 17 > 0 and 8x + 3 < 0) or (25x + 17 < 0 and 8x + 3 > 0)
[{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}<0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a > 0 and b < 0) or (a < 0 and b > 0)]
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x>\frac{-17}{25} \text { and } x<\frac{-3}{8} \right){/tex} or {tex}\left(x<\frac{-17}{25} \text { and } x>\frac{-3}{8}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{-17}{25}<x<\frac{-3}{8}{/tex} [Since x < {tex}\frac{-17}{25}{/tex} and x > {tex}\frac{-3}{8}{/tex} is not possible]

questions.explanation:
{tex}0<\frac{-x}{2}<3{/tex} 
Multiplying throughout  by 2, we get
0 < -x < 6

questions.question: Identify solution set for |4 - x| + 1 < 3?
questions.question_raw: Identify solution set for |4 - x| + 1 < 3?
questions.explanation:
|4 - x| + 1 < 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |4 - x| < 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 < 4 - x < 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 - 4 < -x < 2 - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -6 < -x < -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 > x > 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\in{/tex} (2, 6)
So solution set is (2, 6)

questions.question: What is the solution set for $\frac{|x-2|}{x-2}>0$?
questions.question_raw: What is the solution set for {tex}\frac{|x-2|}{x-2}>0{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
We have |x - 2| {tex}\ge{/tex} 0  [ {tex}\because {/tex} |x| or absolute value of x is always positive or zero but never negative]
Now {tex}\frac{|x-2|}{x-2}>0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} x - 2 > 0  [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}{/tex} > 0, a {tex}\ge{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b > 0] 
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} x > 2 
{tex}\Rightarrow x\epsilon \left( 2,\infty \right) {/tex}

questions.question: Identify the solution set for $\frac{1}{|x|-3}<\frac{1}{2}$?
questions.question_raw: Identify the solution set for {tex}\frac{1}{|x|-3}<\frac{1}{2}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{|x|-3}<\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{|x|-3}-\frac{1}{2}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2-|x|+3}{2(|x|-3)}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{5-|x|}{(|x|-3)}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (5 - |x| < 0 and |x| - 3 > 0) or (5 - |x| > 0 and |x| - 3 < 0)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (|x| > 5 and |x| > 3) or (|x| < 5 and |x| < 3)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |x| > 5 or |x| < 3 [{tex}\because{/tex} |x| > a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x < -a or x > a and |x| < a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -a < x < a]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x < -5 or x > 5) or -3 < x < 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\epsilon{/tex} ({tex}-\infty{/tex}, -5) or x {tex}\epsilon{/tex} (5, {tex}\infty{/tex}) or x {tex}\epsilon{/tex} (-3, 3)
{tex}\Rightarrow x \epsilon(-\infty,-5) \cup(5, \infty) \cup(-3,3){/tex}

questions.question: What is the solution set for $\left|\frac{2 x-1}{x-1}\right|>2$ ?
questions.question_raw: What is the solution set for {tex}\left|\frac{2 x-1}{x-1}\right|>2{/tex} ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\left|\frac{2 x-1}{x-1}\right|>2{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow { { { \frac { 2x-1 }{ x-1 } >2 } } }\quad or\quad \frac { 2x-1 }{ x-1 } <-2\quad \quad \quad \quad \quad \left[ \because \left| x \right| >a\Rightarrow x<-a\quad or\quad x>a \right] {/tex}

First consider {tex} \frac { 2x-1 }{ x-1 } >2{/tex}

{tex} \frac { 2x-1 }{ x-1 } -2>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1-2(x-1)}{x-1}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1-2 x+2}{x-1}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x-1}>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x-1>0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x>1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \epsilon(1, \infty){/tex}

Now {tex} \frac { 2x-1 }{ x-1 } <-2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1}{x-1}+2<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{2 x-1+2(x-1)}{x-1}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{4 x-3}{x-1}<0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(4 x-3>0 \text { and } x-1<0) \text { or }(4 x-3<0 \text { and } x-1>0){/tex}
{tex}\left[\because \frac{a}{b}<0 \Rightarrow(a>0 \text { and } b<0) \quad \text { or } \quad(a<0 \text { and } \quad b>0)\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left(x>\frac{3}{4} \quad \text { and } \quad x<1 \quad\right) \quad \text { or } \quad\left(x<\frac{3}{4} \quad \text { and } \quad x>1\right){/tex}
{tex}\left[\text { Since } \quad x<\frac{3}{4} \quad \text { and } \quad x>1 \text { is } \quad \text { not } \quad \text { possible }\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \in\left(\frac{3}{4}, 1\right){/tex}

questions.question: In the first three papers each of 100 marks , Rishi got 95 , 72 , 83 marks . If he wants an average of greater than or equal to 75 marks and less than 80 marks, find the range of marks he should score in the 4th paper .
questions.question_raw: In the first three papers each of 100 marks , Rishi got 95 , 72 , 83 marks . If he wants an average of greater than or equal to 75 marks and less than 80 marks, find the range of marks he should score in the 4th paper .
questions.explanation:
Let the marks scored by Rishi in the fourth paper be x.

Then{tex}75\le \frac { 95+72+83+x }{ 4 } <80{/tex} 
{tex}\\ \Rightarrow 75\le \frac { 250+x }{ 4 } <80{/tex}

Multipling the inequality throughout by 4 ,we get
{tex}300\le 250+x<320{/tex}
{tex}\\ \Rightarrow 300-250\le 250+x-250<320-250{/tex}

{tex}\\ \Rightarrow 50\le x<70{/tex}

questions.question: A man wants to cut three lengths from a single piece of board of length 91 cm. The second length is to be 3 cm longer than the shortest and third length is to be twice as long as the shortest. What are the possible lengths for the shortest board if the third piece is to be at least 5 cm longer than the second?
questions.question_raw: A man wants to cut three lengths from a single piece of board of length 91 cm. The second length is to be 3 cm longer than the shortest and third length is to be twice as long as the shortest. What are the possible lengths for the shortest board if the third piece is to be at least 5 cm longer than the second?
questions.explanation:
Let the length of the shortest piece be x cm. Then we have the length of the second and third pieces are x + 3 and 2x centimeters respectively.
According to the question,
x + (x + 3) + 2x {tex}\le{/tex} 91
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x + 3 {tex}\le{/tex} 91
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x {tex}\le{/tex} 88
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\le{/tex} 22
Also 2x {tex}\ge{/tex} (x + 3) + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x {tex}\ge{/tex} x + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\ge{/tex} 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 {tex}\le{/tex} x {tex}\le{/tex} 22
Hence the shortest piece may be atleast 8 cm long but it cannot be more than 22cm in length.

questions.question: Find all pairs of consecutive odd natural numbers, both of which are larger than 10, such that their sum is less than 40.
questions.question_raw: Find all pairs of consecutive odd natural numbers, both of which are larger than 10, such that their sum is less than 40.
questions.explanation:
Let the consecutive odd natural numbers be x and x + 2.
According to the question, x > 10 and x + (x + 2) < 40 
Now x + (x + 2) < 40 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x < 38 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x < 19
Hence the maximum value of x is 17 and minimum value is 11.
So the possible pairs of odd natural numbers are (11, 13), (13, 15), (15, 17), (17, 19)

questions.question: The longest side of a triangle is three times the shortest side and the third side is 2 cm shorter than the longest side if the perimeter of the triangles is at least 61 cm, find the minimum length of the shortest side.
questions.question_raw: The longest side of a triangle is three times the shortest side and the third side is 2 cm shorter than the longest side if the perimeter of the triangles is at least 61 cm, find the minimum length of the shortest side.
questions.explanation:
Let the shortest side of a triangle be x cm. Then the length of the longest side is 3x cm and the length of the third side is (3x - 2) cm.
Given the perimeter of the triangles at least 61cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 3x + (3x - 2) {tex}\ge{/tex} 61
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x - 2 {tex}\ge{/tex} 61
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7x {tex}\ge{/tex} 63
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\ge{/tex} 9 
Hence the minimum length of the shortest side = 9 cm

questions.question: Given that x is an integer, find the values of x which satisfy both 2x + 3 > 7and x + 4 < 10
questions.question_raw: Given that x is an integer, find the values of x which satisfy both 2x + 3 > 7and x + 4 < 10
questions.explanation:
2x + 3 > 7 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x + 3 - 3 > 7 - 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x > 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x > 2
Since x is an integer the solution set = {x : x > 2, x {tex}\in{/tex} Z} = {3, 4, 5, 6, 7 .....}
Now x + 4 < 10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 4 - 4 < 10 - 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x < 6
Since x is an integer the solution set = {x : x > 6, x {tex}\in{/tex} Z} = {.... -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
Hence the integer values which satisfy both the inequalities are {3, 4, 5, 6, 7 .....} {tex}\cap{/tex} {.... -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5} = {3, 4, 5}

Therefore the solution of the simultaneous linear equalities is {tex} - {\text{ 2}} \leq {\text{x}} \leq {\text{5}}{/tex}.

questions.question: Solve the system of inequalities (x + 5) - 7(x - 2) $\ge$ 4x + 9, 2(x - 3) - 7(x + 5) $\le$ 3x - 9
questions.question_raw: Solve the system of inequalities (x + 5) - 7(x - 2) {tex}\ge{/tex} 4x + 9, 2(x - 3) - 7(x + 5) {tex}\le{/tex} 3x - 9
questions.explanation:
(x + 5) - 7(x - 2) {tex}\ge{/tex} 4x + 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 5 - 7x + 14 {tex}\ge{/tex} 4x + 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -6x + 19 {tex}\ge{/tex} 4x + 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -6x - 4x {tex}\ge{/tex} 9 - 19
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -10x {tex}\ge{/tex} -10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\le{/tex} 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x \in(-\infty, 1]{/tex} 
2(x - 3) - 7(x + 5) {tex}\le{/tex} 3x - 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 6 - 7x - 35 {tex}\le{/tex} 3x < 9 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -5x - 41 {tex}\le{/tex} 3x - 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -5x - 3x {tex}\le{/tex} 41 - 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -8x {tex}\le{/tex} 32
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -x {tex}\le{/tex} {tex}\frac{32}{8}{/tex} = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\ge{/tex} -4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x \epsilon[-4, \infty){/tex}
Hence the solution  set is {tex}\left[ -4,\infty \right) \cap { \left( -\infty ,1 \right] }{/tex} = [-4, 1]
Which means {tex} - {\text{ 4 }} \leq {\text{ x }} \leq {\text{ 1}}{/tex}

questions.question: Solve the system of inequalities: x - 5 > 0, $\frac{2 x-4}{x+2}$ < 2
questions.question_raw: Solve the system of inequalities: x - 5 > 0, {tex}\frac{2 x-4}{x+2}{/tex} < 2
questions.explanation:
x - 5 > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x > 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x \in(5, \infty){/tex} 
Now {tex}\frac{2 x-4}{x+2}{/tex} < 2
{tex}\frac{2 x-4}{x+2}{/tex} - 2 < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x-4-2(x+2)}{x+2}{/tex} < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x-4-2 x-4}{x+2}{/tex} < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-8}{x+2}{/tex} < 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + 2 > 0  [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}{/tex} < 0, a < 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b > 0]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x > -2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x \in(-2, \infty){/tex} 
Hence the solution set is {tex}\left( 5,\infty \right) \cap \left( -2,\infty \right) =\left( 5,\infty \right) {/tex} 
Which means x > 5.

questions.question: Solve the system of inequalities: $\frac{x+7}{x-8}>2, \frac{2 x+1}{7 x-1}>5$
questions.question_raw: Solve the system of inequalities: {tex}\frac{x+7}{x-8}>2, \frac{2 x+1}{7 x-1}>5{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac {x + 7} {x - 8} > 2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac {x + 7} {x - 8} -2> 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x+7-2(x-8)}{x-8}{/tex} > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{x+7-2 x+16}{x-8}{/tex} > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(23-x)}{x-8}{/tex} > 0 [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}{/tex} > 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a > 0 and b > 0) or (a < 0 and b < 0)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (23 - x > 0 and x - 8 > 0) or (23 - x < 0 and x - 8 < 0)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x < 23 and x > 8) or (x > 23 and x < 8)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 < x < 23 [Since x > 23 and x < 8 is not possible]
{tex}\Rightarrow x\epsilon \left( 8,23 \right) {/tex}
Now {tex}{{2x + 1} \over {7x - 1}}{/tex} > 5 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac {2x + 1} {7x - 1}{/tex} - 5 > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x+1-5(7 x-1)}{7 x-1}{/tex} > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 x+1-35 x+5}{7 x-1}{/tex} > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{(6-33 x)}{7 x-1}{/tex} > 0 [{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{a}{b}{/tex} > 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (a > 0 and b > 0) or (a < 0 and b < 0)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (6 - 33x > 0 and 7x - 1 > 0) or (6 - 33x < 0 and 7x - 1 < 0)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x < {tex}\frac{6}{33}{/tex} and x > {tex}\frac{1}{7}{/tex}) or (x > {tex}\frac{2}{11}{/tex} and x < {tex}\frac{1}{7}{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{7}<x<\frac{2}{11}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}x \in\left(\frac{1}{7}, \frac{2}{11}\right){/tex} [Since x > {tex}\frac{2}{11}{/tex} and x < {tex}\frac{1}{7}{/tex} is not possible]
Hence, the solution of the system {tex}\frac{x+7}{x-8}>2, \frac{2 x+1}{7 x-1}>5{/tex} will be {tex}\left( 8,23 \right) \cap \left( \frac { 1 }{ 7 } ,\frac { 2 }{ 11 } \right) =\phi {/tex}

questions.question: Find all pairs of consecutive even positive integers, both of which are larger than 5, such that their sum is less than 23.
questions.question_raw: Find all pairs of consecutive even positive integers, both of which are larger than 5, such that their sum is less than 23.
questions.explanation:
Let the consecutive even positive integers be x and x + 2.
By data, x > 5 and x  + (x + 2) < 23
Now x + (x + 2) < 23
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x + 2 < 23
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x < 21
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x < {tex}\frac{21}{2}{/tex} = {tex}10\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
So we have the least possible value of x is 6 and the maximum value of x is 10.
Therefore the possible pairs of consecutive even positive integers are (6, 8), (8, 10), (10, 12).

Given 2(x - 1) < 3x - 1

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -x < + 1

Now 4x - 3 {tex}\leq{/tex} 8 + x

Hence A {tex}\cap{/tex} B = {0, 1, 2, 3}

questions.explanation:
First of all for log(x - 1) to be defined, x − 1 > 0

Now, {tex} \log _{0.3}(x-1)<\log _{0.09}(x-1){/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 2 \log _{0.3}(x-1)<\log _{0.3}(x-1) {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \log _{0.3}(x-1)^2<\log _{0.3}(x-1){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1)2 > (x - 1)
[Here inequality is reversed because base lies between 0 and 1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1)2 - (x - 1) > 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 1)(x - 2) > 0 ...(ii)

questions.question: The largest interval for which x^12 - x^9 + x^4 - x + 1 > 0 is
questions.question_raw: The largest interval for which x12 - x9 + x4 - x + 1 > 0 is

For x < 0, put x = -y where y > 0 then we get
f(x) = {tex}y^{12}+y^9+y^4+y+1>0{/tex}  for y > 0
 For 0 < x < 1, x9 < x4 {tex} \Rightarrow-x^9+x^4>0{/tex}
Also 1 - x > 0 and x12 > 0
{tex}\Rightarrow x^{12}-x^9+x^4+1-x>0 {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} f(x) > 0

questions.question: Let a > 0, b > 0 and c > 0. Then the roots of the equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0
questions.question_raw: Let a > 0, b > 0 and c > 0. Then the roots of the equation ax2 + bx + c = 0

options.text: both are real and negative and have negative real parts

questions.explanation:
Since, a, b,  c > 0; therefore a, b, c should be real because order relation is not defined in the set of complex numbers.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Roots of equation are either real or complex conjugate.
Let {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} be the roots of ax2 + bx + c = 0, then
{tex}\alpha+\beta=-\frac{b}{a}=-v e,{/tex} {tex}\alpha \beta=\frac{c}{a}=+v e{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} Either both {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} are -ve, if roots are real or both {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex} have -ve real parts, if roots are complex conjugate.

questions.question: The number of integral solutions x of $\log _{\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^2 \geq$ 0 is
questions.question_raw: The number of integral solutions x of {tex}\log _{\left(x+\frac{7}{2}\right)}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^2 \geq{/tex} 0 is
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\log _{x+\frac{7}{2}}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^2 \geq 0{/tex}
Where, {tex}\frac{x-7}{2 x-3} \neq 0 \Rightarrow x \neq 7{/tex}
{tex}2 x-3+0 \Rightarrow x \neq 3 / 2{/tex}
Feasible region: {tex}x+\frac{7}{2}>0 \Rightarrow x>-\frac{7}{2}{/tex}
and {tex}x+\frac{7}{2} \neq 1 \Rightarrow x \neq \frac{-5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}x \in\left(\frac{-7}{2}, \infty\right)-\left\{-\frac{5}{2}, \frac{3}{2}, 7\right\}{/tex}
Now, {tex} \log _a b \geq 0{/tex} if a > 1 and {tex} b \geq 1{/tex}  or  a {tex}\in{/tex} (0, 1) and {tex} b \in(0,1){/tex}
Condition-1: {tex}x+\frac{7}{2}>1{/tex} and {tex}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^2 \geq 1{/tex}
{tex}x>1-\frac{7}{2} \Rightarrow x>-\frac{5}{2}{/tex} and {tex}(2 x-3)^2-(x-7)^2 \geq 0{/tex} {tex}(3 x-10)(x+4) \leq 0{/tex}
Then, {tex}x \in\left[-4, \frac{10}{3}\right]{/tex}
Intersection: {tex}x \in\left(\frac{-5}{2}, \frac{10}{3}\right]{/tex}
Condition-2: {tex}x+\frac{7}{2} \in(0,1){/tex} and {tex}\left(\frac{x-7}{2 x-3}\right)^2 \in(0,1){/tex}
{tex}0<x+\frac{7}{2}<1 \Rightarrow-\frac{7}{2}<x<\frac{-5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}-7}{2 \mathrm{x}-3}\right)^2<1 \Rightarrow(\mathrm{x}-7)^2<(2 \mathrm{x}-3)^2{/tex}
{tex}x \in(-\infty,-4) \cup\left(\frac{10}{3}, \infty\right){/tex}
There is no common values of x.
We get {tex}x \in\left(\frac{-5}{2}, \frac{10}{3}\right]-\left\{\frac{3}{2}\right\}{/tex}
Integral value of x are {-2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3}
No. of integral values = 6

questions.question: If a_1, a_2 ..., a_n are positive real numbers whose product is a fixed number c, then the minimum value of a_1 + a_2 +...+ a_n-1 + 2a_n is
questions.question_raw: If a1, a2 ..., an are positive real numbers whose product is a fixed number c, then the minimum value of a1 + a2 +...+ an-1 + 2an is
questions.explanation:
Since for positive real numbers,
{tex}\text { A.M. } \geq \text { GM. }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{\left(a_1+a_2+\ldots+a_{n-1}+2 a_n\right)}{n} \geq\left(a_1 a_2 \ldots a_{n-1} 2 a_n\right)^{1 / n}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  a1 + a2 + a3 +...+ an-1 + 2 {tex}a_n \geq n(2 c)^{1 / n}{/tex}
Hence, required minimum value = n(2c)1/n
